name: Rolling The Die
subtitle: The only die you need
body: Roll one six-sided die when you try anything hard. That's it. Higher is better. A 1 is always disaster.
**3–6** — Success, if you have a fitting ability.
**5–6** — Success, if you do not.
**1** — You Forget How. Slapstick. The GM decides how badly.

name: You Forget How
subtitle: Rolling a 1
body: A 1 is always disaster. You don't just fail — you slapstick. The GM narrates the humiliation.
Example: roll a 1 while climbing the bee tree and you Forget How halfway up — narrate the fall.

name: Fight Rolls
subtitle: Hitting things

meta.Defense: Target's Evade/Armor subtracts from your roll

body: Roll 1d6 and meet your Attack value (5, or 4 if you are a Banging caveman). Subtract the target's Evade or Armor from your roll. If you still meet your Attack, you hit and deal your Damage.
There is no AC, no spell slots, no skill list as long as your arm. There is a caveman, three or four words, a rock, and a die that hates you.

name: Ungggh Points
subtitle: Hit points, caveman style
body: You take damage as lost Ungggh Points. Grunt vigorously when you lose them.
Hit 0 → you flop over, dazed. Nobody dies easily — that's the point.
A night in the home cave heals you fully. Cavemen heal like cartoons.

name: Rest And Healing
body: One evening in the home cave restores full Ungggh Points. Cavemen heal like cartoons.

name: The 18 Words
subtitle: You speak ONLY these

meta.The words: You · Me · Rock · Water · Fire · Stick · Hairy · Bang · Sleep · Smelly · Small · Big · Cave · Food · Thing · Shiny · Go · Verisimilitude

body: Your sheet circles the few words you know. No "out of character" talk — just your circled words, grunts, gestures, and stick-figure drawings.
When stuck for words, say them louder. It always helps.
A clever 10-step plan will not survive contact with your vocabulary. This is correct and good.

name: Forced Resolution
subtitle: When the grunting circles the drain
body: Seven people with three words each, all miming at once, is gloriously incomprehensible — for about ninety seconds. After that it is just noise.

The instant two players have grunted "Go bang shiny" at each other three times, step in. Every player with a plan rolls 1d6 (Eloquent adds +1). Highest roll is Leader Of The Moment; everyone else falls in line and does as the leader *seems* to say. Reroll ties.
Do this early, do this often.

name: Run Away
subtitle: Free for everyone
body: Run Away is free — everyone has it, and fleeing is usually the smartest move you can make.
A Fast caveman succeeds at Run Away on anything but a 1.

name: Caveman Abilities

meta.Known abilities: Run Away · Forage · Explore · Intimidate · Lift · Throw · Climb · Build · Fire · Draw · Cook · Fish · Hunt · Tiptoe · Jump · Hide · Resistance

body: If a fitting ability applies, you succeed on 3–6. Without one, you need 5–6. Everyone gets Run Away free.
**Build, Fire, and Draw are exclusive** — only a Learned caveman can do them at all.
Resistance (Tough) succeeds on anything but a 1 — shrugs off leech venom, sleep-grogginess, bad ideas.

name: Making A New Caveman
subtitle: Or your replacement after you get eaten

meta.Class: Eloquent · Strong · Learned · Fast · Tough · Banging · Grunting

body: Pick one class, then roll: 1d6+2 words (Eloquent 1d6+4), 1d6+3 Ungggh Points (Tough 1d6+6), and pick 3 abilities (Learned picks 6). Everyone gets Run Away free.
Circle your words from the 18. You are a caveman. Not the kind who invents fire and civilization. The other kind. Go get eaten gloriously.

name: Word Awards
subtitle: Session-end reward
body: Award one new word at session end:
- SLEEP — for anyone who got rayed
- SHINY — the workhorse choice
- GO — if the saucer left an impression
- Resist the urge to hand out "spaceship"
- The drabbest useful word beats the coolest one
- Whatever names their dumbest moment

name: Technology Sucks
body: Reward cleverness when a Learned caveman Builds something — then have a clanmate smash it on sight. Technology Sucks.

name: Sleep Ray
body: The ray gun sleeps, it doesn't kill. Sleep-rayed cavemen drop for 1d6 rounds, then wake groggy — "for the specimen's own safety."
A Tough caveman's Resistance shrugs off the grogginess on anything but a 1.

name: Tar Pits
body: Instant death. Only spring them when players have had multiple clear chances to avoid them. They ring the Stinky Water Place.
Luring something INTO the tar, on the other hand, is excellent caveman problem-solving.
